judgment.summary: Twilio exhibits strong top-line growth and impressive recent earnings acceleration, but faces valuation concerns and a highly leveraged balance sheet.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported quarter (ending 2026-03-31) EPS of $1.50 beat estimates of $1.27 by 18.0%. Next earnings date is 2026-04-30.
positives: Revenue grew 20.0% year-over-year, indicating continued market demand.
Earnings growth of 375.0% year-over-year and quarterly earnings growth of 350.3% show significant profitability improvements.
The PEG ratio of 0.37 suggests the stock may be undervalued relative to its expected earnings growth.
Strong current and quick ratios (4.66 and 4.13 respectively) indicate excellent short-term liquidity.
Free cash flow of $879.87M provides ample operational cash generation.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 286.43 is exceptionally high, suggesting a very expensive valuation on current earnings.
Forward P/E of 29.09, while much lower, still prices in substantial future growth.
Profit margin of 2.0% and Return on Equity of 1.3% are quite low.
Debt/Equity ratio of 13.72 is very high, indicating significant financial leverage.
Beta of 1.32 suggests higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: Twilio (TWLO) is currently trading around $191.69. Recent analyst reports from Oppenheimer highlight broad-based demand across core services and growing agentic opportunities, leading to an increased price target of $235 from $200 with an Outperform rating. However, some news indicates that a fund fully exited a $4 million stake, and the stock has lagged the market, with one fund dumping a $4.6 million stake. The company has also unveiled an expanded AI customer engagement suite.
